sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: V1 release checklist for cortex. Core features are built — indexer (Python + JS/TS scanning), MCP server, viz dashboard. This document tracks everything that must be done before V1 ships: bugs to fix, improvements to land, audits to complete, code changes, and E2E acceptance stories that become T3 tests.

sections.heading: Execution Plan
sections.content:
Phased ordering — each phase builds on the one before it.

| Phase | Items | Why this order |
|-------|-------|----------------|
| **1 — Foundation** | Staleness rename + A5 (deprecate test runner) | Clean names across the codebase; remove dead code/docs before audits waste time reviewing them |
| **2 — Tooling** | MCP tool gaps (`delete_section`, `delete_doc`, `delete_link`, `list_tags`, `update_section` enhancements, search tag filter) | Build the tools that tag audit and consumer docs need |
| **3 — Audit** | A4 (tag cleanup) + A1 (step-to-line audit) + A2 (annotation audit) + A6 (MCP tool consistency) | Metadata and quality cleanup — these touch different parts and can partially parallelize |
| **4 — Validation** | A3 (test/doc alignment) + E2E acceptance stories | Prove the system works end-to-end; fix drift discovered by audits |
| **5 — Consumer** | Transitive LINKED_STALE + consumer docs site | Provenance chain enables consumer doc staleness tracking; then write Getting Started, Features, Config |
| **6 — Polish** | B1–B12 bugs + I1–I9 improvements | Viz/UI polish on a stable, fully documented system |

sections.heading: Code Changes
sections.content: Required code work before V1 ships.

sections.heading: Staleness Rename
sections.content:
- [ ] CONTENT_STALE → CONTENT_UPDATED
- [ ] DESC_STALE → DESC_UPDATED
- [ ] STRUCTURAL_DRIFT → NOT_FOUND
- [ ] Update staleness engine, MCP server, check output, tests, docs

sections.heading: MCP Tool Gaps
sections.content:
- [ ] `delete_section` — destructive, needs re-index
- [ ] `delete_link` — no other way to remove a link
- [ ] `delete_doc` — no other way to remove a doc
- [ ] `list_tags` — discoverability for agents
- [ ] `update_section` — add `after`/`parent_id` for move, `tags` for section tagging
- [ ] `cortex_search` — add tag filter
- [ ] `cortex_source` — batch mode: accept list of node_ids, return all sources in one call (Builder reads 3-6 functions per edit)
- [ ] `cortex_graph` — batch mode: accept list of node_ids, return combined graph (check callers/deps for multiple nodes at once)
- [ ] `cortex_read_doc` — multi-section mode: accept list of section slugs, return all in one call (orchestrator reads 5+ sections sequentially)

sections.heading: Transitive LINKED_STALE
sections.content: - [ ] Extend `_get_linked_stale_ids` to follow doc-to-doc `documents` edges (enables consumer doc provenance chain)

sections.heading: Consumer Documentation Site
sections.content:
- [ ] Site-map DocJSON format
- [ ] `cortex render-site` CLI command
- [ ] MkDocs base config
- [ ] Consumer docs: Getting Started, Features Overview, Configuration
- [ ] PRD: `cortex::docs.features.consumer-docs.prd`

sections.heading: Bugs
sections.content: Viz dashboard bugs to fix before V1.

sections.heading: B1: Refresh button method not allowed
sections.content:
**Area:** Doc Manager

The refresh button in the doc manager sends a request the server rejects with "method not allowed".

sections.heading: B2: Bullet indentation lost in saved view
sections.content:
**Area:** Doc Manager

Bullet points render indented correctly in the Tiptap editor, but display flat (no indentation) in the saved/read view.

sections.heading: B3: TOC sidebar missing
sections.content:
**Area:** Doc Manager

The section TOC that showed the document's sections in the docs view is no longer present. Needs to be restored.

sections.heading: B4: Sticky header missing
sections.content:
**Area:** Doc Manager

Top options (refresh, add new file, view source, file title) scroll away when scrolling in the doc view. These should be sticky/fixed at the top. Same for the TOC sidebar — it should not scroll with the main content.

sections.heading: B5: Source button does nothing
sections.content:
**Area:** Detail Panel

Clicking the source button in the detail panel has no effect.

sections.heading: B6: Test nodes not visible in graph
sections.content:
**Area:** Graph View

Test nodes don't appear in the graph view. May be filtered out by the API or the frontend.

sections.heading: B7: Sidebar filter panel not in dark mode
sections.content:
**Area:** Tests & Workflows Tabs

The left sidebar with filters in the tests and workflows tabs is white instead of following the dark theme.

sections.heading: B8: Step-to-line mappings often wrong
sections.content:
**Area:** Workflow View

The workflow view frequently maps dFlow steps to incorrect source line numbers. Needs a full audit.

sections.heading: B9: CLEAN vs VERIFIED color confusion
sections.content:
**Area:** Staleness Display

The CLEAN marker is muted green while VERIFIED is bright green. The distinction is unclear for users.

sections.heading: B10: Blank space at bottom of screen
sections.content:
**Area:** All Tabs

~1-2 inches of dead space at the bottom across all tabs on a 27" screen.

sections.heading: B11: Sidebar scroll isolation broken
sections.content:
**Area:** All Tabs

When the mouse is over the sidebar or a region with its own scroll box, scrolling does not propagate to the main content. The sidebar should be fixed/independent and not scroll with the page. Related to B10.

sections.heading: B12: Flash of wrong project name on refresh
sections.content:
**Area:** All Tabs

On page refresh, project name briefly shows "cortex viz" instead of "cortex", filter options go blank (just headers), then resolves back. Likely a race condition — the app renders before the `/api/meta` or `/api/projects` response arrives, showing defaults or the viz module name instead of the active project.

sections.heading: Improvements
sections.content: Feature improvements to land before V1.

sections.heading: I1: Show links TO and FROM node
sections.content:
**Area:** Detail Panel — Links Section

The links section should display both inbound and outbound edges for the selected node.

sections.heading: I2: Collapsible link groups
sections.content:
**Area:** Detail Panel — Links Section

Links section should be collapsible to allow navigation without clutter.

sections.heading: I3: Link click navigation
sections.content:
**Area:** Detail Panel — Links Section

Clicking a link should navigate contextually:
- Doc link (DocJSON section) → opens doc manager with that file and section loaded
- Code node link → opens list view with the source already loaded for that node

sections.heading: I4: Group by module by default
sections.content:
**Area:** Tests & Workflows Tabs

Tests and workflows should be grouped by module on load. Remove the "none" (ungrouped) option.

sections.heading: I5: Collapsed by default
sections.content:
**Area:** Tests & Workflows Tabs

All module groups should start collapsed.

sections.heading: I6: Collapse All button
sections.content:
**Area:** Tests & Workflows Tabs

Add a button to re-collapse all groups after expanding.

sections.heading: I7: Improve history since filter
sections.content:
**Area:** History Panel

Currently only shows the last commit, but feature branches often have multiple commits. Needs brainstorming on how to make the history view more useful (e.g., show all commits in a range, group by branch, expand/collapse).

sections.heading: I8: Semantic search toggle
sections.content:
**Area:** List & Graph Views

Add a search mode selector: Keyword (default, FTS), Semantic (embedding-based), or Both (merged/deduplicated). Infrastructure already exists in `cortex_search` mode parameter. Could also add filter chips for node type, staleness status, and tags alongside search.

sections.heading: I9: Subsection visual model in doc manager
sections.content:
**Area:** Doc Manager

Subsections currently render with the full overhead of a top-level section — links, metadata, spacing — just indented. This adds excessive whitespace and breaks the visual continuity that subsections are supposed to provide.

Needed:
- **Compact subsection rendering** — Subsections should look more like headers within their parent, not standalone sections. Reduce the chrome (link badges, extra padding) and visually contain them within the parent section boundary.
- **Empty parent sections** — When a section has no content but has subsections, it currently shows an empty box with all the overhead, then indented boxes below. Should collapse the empty parent into just a heading with its children flowing naturally underneath.
- **Collapsible sections** — Allow sections (and subsection groups) to be collapsed/expanded, so large documents like the V1 release checklist are navigable without endless scrolling.

sections.heading: Audits
sections.content: Verification and cleanup work before V1.

sections.heading: A1: Workflow step-to-line audit
sections.content: Systematically verify all step-to-line-number mappings in the workflow view. Fix the scanner or the rendering logic wherever they diverge.

sections.heading: A2: Test and workflow annotation audit
sections.content: Review all tests and workflows to ensure they properly adhere to the test annotation policy and use AutoSteps effectively to make workflows as informative as possible.

sections.heading: A3: Test and doc alignment audit
sections.content: Verify all tests match current behavior and all docs match current code. Clean up stale references, dead feature docs, and broken assumptions.

sections.heading: A4: Tag cleanup
sections.content: Wipe all existing tags from DocJSON docs. Define and enforce a standard tag vocabulary for agent and human use. Keep tags clean and searchable.

sections.heading: A5: Deprecate test runner
sections.content:
Remove `cortex test` execution machinery. Keep test discovery and the viz test tab (read-only viewing of test nodes, tiers, annotations, validates edges, step narratives).

**Remove:**
- `cortex test` CLI command — `cortex::cortex.cli::cmd_test`
- `cortex/index/runner.py` — `cortex::cortex.index.runner`
- `cortex/index/junit_parser.py` — `cortex::cortex.index.junit_parser`
- `cortex/index/test_mapper.py` — `cortex::cortex.index.test_mapper`
- `test_runs` / `test_results` DB tables — `cortex::cortex.index.db::insert_test_run`
- Viz endpoint `get_test_runs()` — `cortex::cortex.viz.server::get_test_runs`
- Viz UI: Run Tests button, pass/fail badges, test run history panel

**Keep:**
- Scanner indexing test nodes (tiers, purpose, steps, validates edges)
- Viz test tab for browsing test annotations
- `validates` edges in the graph

**Docs to update:**
- `cortex::docs.features.indexer.sub_features.test-runner.prd` (already has deprecation banner)
- `cortex::docs.features.indexer.sub_features.test-runner.design` (already has deprecation banner)
- References to `cortex test` in `cortex::docs.features.indexer.interfaces.cli`, `cortex::docs.features.indexer.prd`, `cortex::docs.architecture`

sections.heading: A6: MCP tool consistency audit
sections.content:
Review all MCP tools for consistent logic, error handling, parameter naming, and return format. Ensure tools follow the same patterns for project_root validation, node resolution, scope filtering, and result formatting. Fix any divergences.

**Scope:**
- `cortex::cortex.mcp_server` — all tool handler functions
- Parameter naming conventions (e.g. `node_id` vs `section_id` vs `doc_id`)
- Error messages and failure modes
- Return format consistency (header lines, count reporting, cap/offset behavior)
- Docstring quality and accuracy (these surface as tool descriptions to agents)

sections.heading: E2E Acceptance Stories
sections.content: These become T3 integration tests. V1 ships when all pass.

sections.heading: E1: Edit function body shows staleness cascade
sections.content: As a developer, I want to edit a function body and run `cortex check` so that I can see which node is CONTENT_UPDATED, which doc sections are LINKED_STALE because they describe that function, and that the parent module inherited the worst child status — so I know exactly what docs need reviewing before I commit.

sections.heading: E2: Rename function preserves history
sections.content: As a developer, I want to rename a function and run `cortex build` so that the old node shows NOT_FOUND, the new node appears with correct edges, and I can trace the rename through `cortex history` — so I don't lose track of what changed and the graph stays accurate.

sections.heading: E3: New TypeScript file indexed with edges
sections.content: As a developer, I want to add a new TypeScript file to my project, add its path to `js_paths`, and run `cortex build` so that all its functions appear as nodes and its ESM imports produce dependency edges — so the frontend code is tracked the same way Python code is.

sections.heading: E4: Multi-commit feature branch report
sections.content: As a developer, I want to run `cortex report --since-sha=<branch-start>` after making several commits on a feature branch so that I can see every code and doc change grouped by node across the full branch — so I can verify the work aligns with my intent before merging.

sections.heading: E5: Post-merge staleness check
sections.content: As a developer, I want to merge a feature branch and run `cortex check` so that I can see exactly which nodes changed and which docs need updating — so nothing falls through the cracks post-merge.

sections.heading: E6: Spec-code alignment verification
sections.content: As a developer, I want to update a design spec section and then query its linked code nodes via `cortex_graph` so that I can see whether the code matches what I just wrote — if the code is CLEAN and the spec is CONTENT_UPDATED, I know the spec drifted ahead of the code and I need to either update the code or revert the spec.

sections.heading: E7: Consumer doc transitive staleness
sections.content: As a developer, I want to write a consumer-facing doc section that links to a dev spec section so that when the dev spec's linked code changes in the future, both the dev spec AND my consumer doc flag as needing review — so published documentation never silently drifts from reality.

sections.heading: E8: Agent task flow end-to-end
sections.content: As an AI agent, I want to receive a task, use `cortex_search` to find relevant nodes, `cortex_graph` to trace their dependencies, and `cortex_source` to read the actual code, then after implementing changes run `cortex_check` to verify only expected nodes went stale — so I can confidently complete work without introducing untracked side effects.

sections.heading: E9: Agent mark-clean resets baseline
sections.content: As an AI agent, I want to review stale nodes after a code change and run `cortex mark-clean` with a reason for each one so that subsequent `cortex check` shows them as CLEAN — so the next agent or developer starts from a verified baseline, not a wall of stale warnings.

sections.heading: E10: Graph view full navigation
sections.content: As a developer, I want to open the graph view and see all node types — code modules, functions, doc sections, and tests — with staleness coloring, then click a node to see its detail panel with links to and from it, and navigate directly to the doc manager or source view from those links — so I can visually explore the codebase and its documentation without switching tools.

sections.heading: E11: Semantic search discovery
sections.content: As a developer, I want to search for a concept in the list view using semantic search so that I find relevant nodes even when I don't know the exact function name — so discovery works the way I think, not just the way I type.

sections.heading: E12: Doc editing with sticky TOC
sections.content: As a developer, I want to open the docs tab, see the section TOC, scroll through a document with sticky headers, and edit a section inline so that doc maintenance is fast and I never lose my place — so I actually keep docs up to date instead of deferring it.

sections.heading: E13: LINKED_STALE propagates doc-to-code
sections.content: As a developer, I want to edit a function body that a doc section links to via a `documents` edge, and run `cortex check`, so that the doc section shows LINKED_STALE — so I know which documentation describes code that just changed and may need updating.

sections.heading: E14: LINKED_STALE propagates test-to-code
sections.content: As a developer, I want to edit a function body that a test validates via a `validates` edge, and run `cortex check`, so that the test shows LINKED_STALE — so I know which tests cover code that changed and may need re-running or updating.

sections.heading: E15: Composite inherits worst child staleness
sections.content: As a developer, I want a module node to inherit the worst staleness status from its child functions, so that when one function is CONTENT_UPDATED the module shows CONTENT_UPDATED, and when a child's doc section is LINKED_STALE the module shows at least LINKED_STALE — so I can see at a glance which modules are affected without expanding every function.

sections.heading:
E16: Workflow staleness via step delegation",
<parameter name="content">As a developer, I want to edit a function that a dFlow AutoStep delegates to, and see the step go LINKED_STALE and the parent workflow inherit that staleness — so that `cortex check` tells me which workflows are affected by a code change, without me having to trace the delegation chain manually. (Post-unification, per ADR-005 node integration model.)
